source_document: California SB 1047 (Safe and Secure Innovation for Frontier AI Models Act)
passage_text: "Covered model" means an artificial intelligence model that is trained using a quantity of computing power greater than 10^26 integer or floating-point operations, or that is created by fine-tuning a covered model using a quantity of computing power greater than three times 10^25 integer or floating-point operations. The cost of the quantity of computing power used to train the model, as determined based on commercially available prices at the time of training, is greater than one hundred million dollars ($100,000,000).

definition.definition_text: an artificial intelligence model that is trained using a quantity of computing power greater than 10^26 integer or floating-point operations, or that is created by fine-tuning a covered model using a quantity of computing power greater than three times 10^25 integer or floating-point operations
definition.scope: Models meeting compute threshold or fine-tuning threshold with cost exceeding $100M

evidence_spans.text: an artificial intelligence model that is trained using a quantity of computing power greater than 10^26 integer or floating-point operations

threshold_exception.threshold_condition: computing power used to train the model is greater than 10^26 operations, or fine-tuning exceeds 3×10^25 operations, and cost exceeds $100,000,000

evidence_spans.text: greater than 10^26 integer or floating-point operations

cost of the quantity of computing power used to train the model, as determined based on commercially available prices at the time of training, is greater than one hundred million dollars ($100,000,000)
